questions.text: Which tissue transports water from roots to leaves?
questions.explanation: Xylem vessels conduct water and dissolved minerals upward.

questions.text: The movement of water through xylem driven by evaporation from leaves is called:
questions.explanation: Transpiration pull helps draw water upward through the xylem.

questions.text: Which tissue transports organic food in plants?
questions.explanation: Phloem transports sugars and other organic nutrients.
Root pressure is generated by the inward flow of water into root xylem.

questions.text: Which plant cells help control the opening of stomata?
questions.explanation: Guard cells surround stomatal pores and regulate gas exchange.

questions.text: Translocation in plants refers to the movement of:
questions.explanation: Translocation moves sugars and other organic solutes through phloem.
